brand_dna.category: Historic Spring Mountain District estate winery and luxury visitor destination - selling estate-grown Napa wines alongside tours, tastings, and membership rather than a shelf of SKUs.
brand_dna.core_belief: The epic beauty of this land should show up in every glass. A restored Napa icon, earned by those who dare to dream, where nature and nurture meet grit and grandeur.
proof_points: Founded on three 19th-century estates - La Perla, Chevalier, and Miravalle - established between 1872 and 1884 on the slopes of Spring Mountain.
Spring Mountain Chardonnay competed in the 1976 Judgment of Paris, finishing ahead of famous French houses and helping put Napa on the world map.
225-plus acres of terraced vineyard climbing from roughly 400 to 1,700 feet across an estate of over 800 acres.
The Victorian mansion (Miravalle) served as the exterior of the CBS drama Falcon Crest for much of the 1980s.
Deep library of estate reds spanning multiple decades, poured in the Founders Tasting.
brand_dna.positioning: Top of the market. A heritage-rich, terroir-first mountain estate positioned as a rare destination and collector's wine, not a valley-floor tasting room.

voice_pillars.name: Reverent About the Land
voice_pillars.desc: Frames the wine as an expression of a specific, dramatic mountain terroir rather than a product.
voice_pillars.quote: The epic beauty of this land shows up in every glass.

voice_pillars.name: Heritage-Anchored
voice_pillars.desc: Leans on real, datable history - founding estates, Judgment of Paris, decades of library vintages - to earn authority.
voice_pillars.quote: Founded 1872 to 1884 on the slopes of Spring Mountain.

voice_pillars.name: Understated Luxury
voice_pillars.desc: Speaks quietly and precisely, pairing evocative ideas rather than shouting offers or discounts.
voice_pillars.quote: Grit and grandeur. Precision and perfection. Sensory and soul.

voice_pillars.name: Invitation, Not Hard Sell
voice_pillars.desc: Positions every touchpoint as an invitation to discover the estate, letting the place do the selling.

phrases: library vintages you cannot buy on a shelf
up the mountain, above the fog

icp: Affluent wine lovers, collectors, and luxury travelers - many 45-plus - who value heritage, provenance, and rare access, and who will pay a premium for an estate experience and allocation rather than a supermarket bottle.

personas.name: The Collector
personas.description: A serious cellar-builder moved by library depth, decades of estate vintages, and the Spring Mountain District pedigree - buys allocation and books the Founders Tasting.

personas.name: The Napa Pilgrim
personas.description: An affluent traveler planning a Napa trip who wants a rare, above-the-valley-floor experience - moved by the ridge-top Explorers tour and the estate's beauty and privacy.

personas.name: The Story Seeker
personas.description: A culturally curious visitor drawn to the history - Judgment of Paris, the founding estates, and the Falcon Crest era - who wants to taste where a piece of wine history was made.

offers: Daily estate tours and tastings ranging from $95 to $395 per guest.
The Founders Tasting: a guided tour plus a flight spanning three distinct decades from the library ($200 per guest).
Spring Mountain Explorers: a roughly three-hour off-road estate tour to the high blocks with library and new-release wines ($395 per guest, two departures daily).
Connoisseurs and Collectors membership for allocation and estate access.
Private tastings and estate events by reservation.

allowed.claim: Spring Mountain Chardonnay competed in the 1976 Judgment of Paris and finished ahead of several established French houses.
allowed.source: Estate history / Judgment of Paris record

allowed.claim: The estate was founded on three 19th-century properties - La Perla, Chevalier, and Miravalle - established between 1872 and 1884.
The Founders Tasting pours estate wine spanning three distinct decades from the library.
The estate spans 225-plus acres of vineyard from roughly 400 to 1,700 feet of elevation.
The Miravalle mansion served as the exterior filming location for the CBS series Falcon Crest during the 1980s.

banned.claim: The best wine in Napa / the number one winery on Spring Mountain.
banned.why: Unqualified superlatives that cannot be objectively substantiated are banned under FTC guidance.

banned.claim: Officially partnered with, endorsed by, or affiliated with the Falcon Crest show or CBS.
banned.why: The mansion was only a filming location; implying an official endorsement or partnership is unsupported and misleading.

banned.claim: Our wine beat every French wine at the Judgment of Paris.
banned.why: Spring Mountain's Chardonnay placed in the white flight ahead of several French houses, not first overall; overstating the result is inaccurate.

banned.claim: Health or wellness benefits from drinking the wine.
banned.why: Health claims on an alcoholic beverage are prohibited under TTB and FDA rules.

banned.claim: Limited bottles left - buy in the next X hours.
banned.why: Invented scarcity or urgency is banned; only real, verifiable allocation and availability may be stated.
